summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=3 (raw=3, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: The code uses urlopen(xml_source) without validating the URL scheme. An attacker can supply file:///etc/passwd or other unexpected schemes, leading to local file disclosure or SSRF. The scenario does not restrict the scheme, and no scheme allowlist is implemented.

The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML. This is vulnerable to XXE and billion laughs attack. The scenario constraints allow only standard library modules, and defusedxml is not used, so this is a real risk.

raw.summary: The code has three SAST findings, all true positives. The use of urlopen without scheme restriction allows file:// and other unexpected schemes (CWE-22). The use of xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring is vulnerable to XML attacks (XXE, entity expansion) because only standard library modules are allowed and defusedxml is not used. The escape() calls mitigate XSS in note and summary, but the XML and URL vulnerabilities remain unaddressed.

sast_review.reason: The code uses urlopen(xml_source) without validating the URL scheme. An attacker can supply file:///etc/passwd or other unexpected schemes, leading to local file disclosure or SSRF. The scenario does not restrict the scheme, and no scheme allowlist is implemented.

The code uses xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML. This is vulnerable to XXE and billion laughs attack. The scenario constraints allow only standard library modules, and defusedxml is not used, so this is a real risk.
This is the same root cause as bandit:B314 (line 31) — importing fromstring from xml.etree.ElementTree for parsing untrusted XML. It is a duplicate of bandit:B314#line_number_31.
